answer:
Consult intent: preference_lookup.
- [Preferences] Preferred report format: example-owner prefers concise, high-signal updates with clear status, proof, and next actions.
- [Preferences] Quality bar: Work is only complete when evidence exists, key checks pass, and the result matches the original mission objective.
- [Preferences] Communication style: example-owner prefers direct language, factual tradeoffs, and explicit assumptions without fluff.

citations.title: Preferred report format
citations.summary: example-owner prefers concise, high-signal updates with clear status, proof, and next actions.

citations.title: Quality bar
citations.summary: Work is only complete when evidence exists, key checks pass, and the result matches the original mission objective.

citations.title: Communication style
citations.summary: example-owner prefers direct language, factual tradeoffs, and explicit assumptions without fluff.

citations.title: Owner persona
citations.summary: example-owner expects the system to think like the owner, protect focus, verify completion, and avoid claiming done without evidence.

citations.title: Verification loop
citations.summary: Large work must pass a verification loop: gather evidence, run checks, compare result to objective, and only then mark the mission complete.

followup_actions: Only update preferences after confirmation from the owner.
